scaffolds.q: Glands are organs that produce and release substances.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Glands make things like hormones, sweat, saliva, oil, and digestive enzymes.

scaffolds.q: There are TWO main types of glands: exocrine (with ducts) and endocrine (ductless).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! 'Exo' = outside (secretes to surfaces via ducts). 'Endo' = inside (secretes into blood).

scaffolds.q: EXOCRINE glands have DUCTS that deliver secretions to surfaces.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Sweat glands, salivary glands, digestive glands all have ducts for targeted delivery.

scaffolds.q: ENDOCRINE glands are DUCTLESS - they secrete directly into the bloodstream.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! No pipes needed - hormones go straight into blood for system-wide distribution.

scaffolds.q: Hormones are chemical messengers that travel through the blood.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Endocrine glands make hormones, release them into blood, and let blood carry them everywhere.

scaffolds.q: Examples of endocrine glands: thyroid, pituitary, adrenal glands.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! These all make hormones and release them into the bloodstream.

scaffolds.q: Endocrine glands do NOT secrete mucus - they secrete hormones.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Mucus is an exocrine product (delivered through ducts). Hormones are endocrine products.

scaffolds.q: ENDOCRINE glands secrete HORMONES directly into the BLOODSTREAM.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! No ducts - just direct release into blood for systemic effects.

scaffolds.q: The answer is 'secreting hormones directly into the bloodstream.'
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not having ducts (that's exocrine), not from mesenchyme (wrong origin), not secreting mucus (that's exocrine).
